2026-04-19T06:08:24.2583408Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2583949Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2584371Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2584725Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2585027Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2585254Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2585408Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2585582Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2585745Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2585968Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-19T06:08:24.2586455Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-19T06:08:24.2586717Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. 2026-04-19T06:11:01.7147163Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-19T06:09:31.364436547Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58339,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document x-agent-session-id header and observability features added in Responses beta.2\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58335) by `@azure-sdk`, surfacing changes from PR #58314 by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58314 shipped with the beta.2 release several significant new features:\\n\\n- **`x-agent-session-id` response header** on all protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) per spec §8\\n- **Deterministic session ID derivation** — SHA-256 hash of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` → 63-char hex, with env var and random fallback\\n- **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`** — logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers\\n- **Startup configuration logging** — storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, and event stream TTL logged at startup\\n- **Isolation key presence logging** — `HasUserIsolationKey` / `HasChatIsolationKey` flags in all endpoint logs (values never logged)\\n- **Storage URL masking** — everything before `/storage` is redacted in diagnostic logs; only `api-version` query param retained\\n- **Server version User-Agent** on outbound Foundry storage API requests via `ServerVersionRegistry`\\n- **Inbound request logging** enabled automatically for Tier 1 and Tier 2 setups\\n\\nNone of these features appear in the Responses package README, leaving developers without guidance on the new observability capabilities or the protocol-level `x-agent-session-id` header behavior.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`x-agent-session-id` response header is undocumented** — The README describes `ResponseContext`, streaming modes, and the response lifecycle, but never mentions that every endpoint response includes `x-agent-session-id`. Developers building clients that depend on this header (per spec §8) have no README-level guidance.\\n- **Deterministic session ID derivation not documented** — The algorithm (`\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` → SHA-256 → 63-char hex, with env var / random fallback) is defined in the CHANGELOG but absent from the README\\u0026#39;s Key concepts or Troubleshooting sections.\\n- **Observability / Logging section missing** — The beta.2 release adds a full observability stack (startup logging, per-endpoint structured logging with isolation key presence flags, outbound storage logging with URL masking). The existing Troubleshooting section only mentions OpenTelemetry traces but does not describe the structured log output, what is masked, or how to interpret the new fields.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`  \\n**Action:** Edit — add three new subsections\\n\\n#### 1a. Add `x-agent-session-id` header subsection under \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;\\n\\nInsert after the \\u0026#34;Thread safety\\u0026#34; subsection (just before `## Examples`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Session identity (`x-agent-session-id`)\\n\\nEvery protocol endpoint response includes an `x-agent-session-id` response header. Its value is resolved in this order:\\n\\n1. **Request payload** — `agent_session_id` field in the POST body (if provided)\\n2. **Environment variable** — `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID`\\n3. **Deterministic hash** — SHA-256 of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters, where the partition hint is extracted from `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id`\\n4. **Random** — a random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available\\n\\nThe header is always present on successful responses and on error responses (using the environment variable fallback for errors). Clients can use this header to correlate requests across conversation turns without needing to store the session ID explicitly.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Expand the \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34; subsection under \\u0026#34;Troubleshooting\\u0026#34;\\n\\nReplace the existing two-sentence \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34; paragraph with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\n**Inbound request logging** is enabled automatically for Tier 1 (`ResponsesServer.Run()`) and Tier 2 (`AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`) setups. Each request is logged with HTTP method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). Query strings are excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.\\n\\n**Endpoint structured logging** — all Responses API endpoints emit `Information`-level structured log entries that include the response ID, conversation ID, previous response ID, and isolation key presence flags (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`). Key values are never logged.\\n\\n**Startup configuration logging** — when the host starts, the Responses library logs the configured storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, and event stream TTL at `Information` level.\\n\\n**Foundry storage request logging** — outbound calls to the Foundry storage API are logged via `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` with HTTP method, masked URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers. The URI is masked: everything before `/storage` is replaced with `***`, and all query parameters except `api-version` are stripped. This prevents leaking account, project, and token information in diagnostic output.\\n\\n**OpenTelemetry** — distributed traces are emitted via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source and exported when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for this documentation update. The changes are prose descriptions, not code samples.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` currently has these sections:\\n\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts (ResponseHandler, ResponseContext, ResponseEventStream, Streaming \\u0026amp; Background Modes, Response Lifecycle, Thread safety)\\n3. ❌ **Missing:** Session identity / `x-agent-session-id` — add as new Key concepts subsection (Step 1a above)\\n4. ✅ Examples\\n5. ⚠️ **Incomplete:** Troubleshooting / Logging — expand to cover structured logging, startup logging, storage masking (Step 1b above)\\n6. ✅ Next steps\\n7. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver`\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24622380048/agentic_workflow) · ● 449K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58339\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-19T06:06:49Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-19T06:06:49Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

only `api-version` query param retained\\n- **Server version User-Agent** on outbound Foundry storage API requests via `ServerVersionRegistry`\\n- **Inbound request logging** enabled automatically for Tier 1 and Tier 2 setups\\n\\nNone of these features appear in the Responses package README, leaving developers without guidance on the new observability capabilities or the protocol-level `x-agent-session-id` header behavior.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **`x-agent-session-id` response header is undocumented** — The README describes `ResponseContext`, streaming modes, and the response lifecycle, but never mentions that every endpoint response includes `x-agent-session-id`. Developers building clients that depend on this header (per spec §8) have no README-level guidance.\\n- **Deterministic session ID derivation not documented** — The algorithm (`\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` → SHA-256 → 63-char hex, with env var / random fallback) is defined in the CHANGELOG but absent from the README\\u0026#39;s Key concepts or Troubleshooting sections.\\n- **Observability / Logging section missing** — The beta.2 release adds a full observability stack (startup logging, per-endpoint structured logging with isolation key presence flags, outbound storage logging with URL masking). The existing Troubleshooting section only mentions OpenTelemetry traces but does not describe the structured log output, what is masked, or how to interpret the new fields.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`  \\n**Action:** Edit — add three new subsections\\n\\n#### 1a. Add `x-agent-session-id` header subsection under \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34;\\n\\nInsert after the \\u0026#34;Thread safety\\u0026#34; subsection (just before `## Examples`):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Session identity (`x-agent-session-id`)\\n\\nEvery protocol endpoint response includes an `x-agent-session-id` response header. Its value is resolved in this order:\\n\\n1. **Request payload** — `agent_session_id` field in the POST body (if provided)\\n2. **Environment variable** — `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID`\\n3. **Deterministic hash** — SHA-256 of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters, where the partition hint is extracted from `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id`\\n4. **Random** — a random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available\\n\\nThe header is always present on successful responses and on error responses (using the environment variable fallback for errors). Clients can use this header to correlate requests across conversation turns without needing to store the session ID explicitly.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Expand the \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34; subsection under \\u0026#34;Troubleshooting\\u0026#34;\\n\\nReplace the existing two-sentence \\u0026#34;Logging\\u0026#34; paragraph with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\n**Inbound request logging** is enabled automatically for Tier 1 (`ResponsesServer.Run()`) and Tier 2 (`AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`) setups. Each request is logged with HTTP method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). Query strings are excluded to avoid logging sensitive data.\\n\\n**Endpoint structured logging** — all Responses API endpoints emit `Information`-level structured log entries that include the response ID, conversation ID, previous response ID, and isolation key presence flags (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`). Key values are never logged.\\n\\n**Startup configuration logging** — when the host starts, the Responses library logs the configured storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, and event stream TTL at `Information` level.\\n\\n**Foundry storage request logging** — outbound calls to the Foundry storage API are logged via `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` with HTTP method, masked URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers. The URI is masked: everything before `/storage` is replaced with `***`, and all query parameters except `api-version` are stripped. This prevents leaking account, project, and token information in diagnostic output.\\n\\n**OpenTelemetry** — distributed traces are emitted via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source and exported when `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` is set.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are required for this documentation update.
